title: Auditorium Assistance Request
openingLine: Hey, I'm working on an auditorium renovation at Brookhaven High and I'm a little out of my depth on the stage and acoustic side. I'm sending you a PDF of the space — limited dimensions on it. Can you help me figure out what I should be specifying?
customerContext: You're three years into your role and this is your first big auditorium project. The district hired your firm because of the academic work you've done — they didn't hire a theatre consultant. You're nervous about specifying stage and acoustic systems you don't fully understand and you're hoping the rep can fill the gap without making you look uninformed.

hiddenInfo.reveal: No theatre consultant on the team. District didn't budget for one.
$8.5M auditorium renovation. Stage/acoustic/rigging scope is roughly $1.3M of that.
We have CAD and Revit files but I haven't shared them yet — happy to send them over.
Existing pit but it's never used as a pit anymore. Owner wants it filled and usable as stage extension. No acoustic shell currently — wants one.
The district facilities director and the music/theatre teachers are very involved. They've voiced strong opinions in design meetings.
Bid documents due in 14 weeks. Construction starts next summer.
I've used Wenger products on a small project two years ago — risers only. Never spec'd a full stage system.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked whether a theatre consultant is engaged
Asked about full stage shell needs
Asked about orchestra pit filler scope
Asked if CAD or Revit files are available
Asked about owner involvement and stakeholders
Researched architect's prior Wenger work before the call

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered integration support given there's no theatre consultant
Offered to provide layouts via OppExchange

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Probed for music education and athletics scope on the project
Identified full stage shell and pit filler as expansion opportunities

BANT.behavior: Identified all decision makers (architect, owner, music/theatre staff)

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped Wenger involvement against the 14-week bid doc deadline

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Committed to deliver layout, budget, and specifications
Offered to attend a design meeting in person or virtually
